compiled_state.title: As a developer, hierarchy enforcement is validated by a performance benchmark and dep-graph correctness test
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description
**What**: Write and execute two acceptance tests: (1) A benchmark that builds a synthetic 1,000-ticket tree (epics, stories, tasks in correct hierarchy) and runs representative ticket link operations, asserting all complete in under 5 seconds on a developer workstation. (2) An end-to-end dep-graph correctness test using a real .tickets-tracker/ fixture: create a ticket tree, run a cross-story ticket link, then assert (a) ticket show story-X lists story-X→story-Y in its deps and (b) ticket show task-A does NOT list task-A→task-B in its deps.
**Why**: Proves the feature works at the observable graph-output level (not just exit codes) and meets the latency guarantee.
**Scope**:
- IN: Performance benchmark test (SC13), end-to-end dep-graph assertion test (SC14), real .tickets-tracker/ fixture for integration test
- OUT: Unit tests for individual functions (covered in S1–S3)

## Done Definitions
- When this story is complete, the performance benchmark runs and passes: all ticket link operations on a synthetic 1,000-ticket tree complete in under 5 seconds on a developer workstation
  ← Satisfies: SC13
- When this story is complete, the dep-graph test passes: after a cross-story link promotion, ticket show on the source story lists the promoted story-level dep, and ticket show on the source task does NOT list the original task-level dep
  ← Satisfies: SC14
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations
- [Testing] Benchmark must use a realistic nested hierarchy (epic→story→task) not a flat list, to exercise the full ancestry walk
- [Testing] The dep-graph integration test must use a real .tickets-tracker/ fixture (not mocked) to verify actual event writing and state reduction

## Escalation Policy
**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
